day_beats.story_beat: A kid stops in front of the shelf, mouth open. 'They still make these?' His older sister steers him toward the food court. He looks back twice.
day_beats.forward_hook: Word travels fast. A collector mentioned she'd swing by tomorrow with a specific wish list.

day_beats.story_beat: A woman in a blazer picks up a cartridge, turns it over, sets it back down. She doesn't buy — but she lingers longer than most.
day_beats.forward_hook: The weekend crowd tends to run younger. Keep the counter clear and the prices honest.

day_beats.story_beat: Someone left a handwritten note in the returns bin: 'This game saved my summer. Hope it saves someone else's too.' No name.
day_beats.forward_hook: Rumor from the food court: a reseller is doing the rounds. Price accordingly.

day_beats.story_beat: The mall's overhead PA plays a jingle that hasn't aired since 1998. Three customers stop mid-sentence and just listen.
day_beats.forward_hook: You're starting to see regulars. That's a good sign — it means they trust the stock.

day_beats.story_beat: A teenager haggles for twenty minutes on a ten-dollar game, then buys it at full price anyway. He seems satisfied regardless.
day_beats.forward_hook: The weekend is close. Fridays move more cartridges than any other day of the week.

day_beats.story_beat: Two customers arrive separately, both looking for the same item. One leaves with it. The other takes your number. You didn't know you had a number until you gave it.
day_beats.forward_hook: End of your first week tomorrow. Take stock of what sold and what didn't. The shelf tells a story if you let it.

day_beats.story_beat: A little girl asks if you work here every day. You say yes. She nods like this is important information she will remember. Maybe she will.
day_beats.forward_hook: Week two. The patterns are becoming visible. Stock what moves; rotate what doesn't.

fallback_beat: The mall hums along. Another day logged.
fallback_hook: Tomorrow brings fresh inventory and new faces.
